response_constraints: Ground every reported ID, date, and update outcome in the corresponding tool response.
In the final summary, give the original, interim, and corrected dates in that turn order.
Treat 2026-12-12 as the final date and 2026-08-12 only as the superseded interim date.
Do not claim that a notification, cancellation, recreation, profile lookup, list, availability check, or unrelated-record update occurred.

stages.purpose: Resolve one stable ID and its original date from the user's name-and-location description before any mutation.
Apply the first date instruction only after the target ID is established in the prior turn.
Apply the correction to the same stable ID; the later instruction supersedes the earlier date.

follow_up_turns.content: That is the title I meant. Change only its date to 2026-08-12. Do not touch either nearby record and do not notify anyone.
Correction: replace my previous date with 2026-12-12 for that same title. This supersedes 2026-08-12; do not cancel or recreate the record, and still send no notification.

prompt: I’m reconciling the title filed as “River Almanac” at Central Branch, but I do not know its record ID. Locate it and tell me the ID and current date from the search result. This first turn is read-only: do not open another record, edit, cancel, create, or notify anything. In the final summary, give the original, interim, and corrected dates in that turn order.
